_comment: Round 2: ambiguous, longer-horizon task. Cases store the CANONICAL resolved values (account_id, product, quantity); the inbox text expresses them ambiguously (vague quantities, multiple account ids, narrative red herrings). seed_db.py, mock_api.py, and analysis/ground_truth.py derive from this.

rules.escalate_if: tier == 'Enterprise' AND total > escalation_threshold  (total is POST-discount)
rules.account_selection: use the account being billed / placing the order; ignore shipping, parent, or merely-referenced accounts
rules.quantity_interpretation: natural language ('a dozen'=12, 'two dozen'=24); a range uses the lower bound; if unstated, assume 1

case01.note: Range qty 'between 20 and 25' -> lower bound 20 (triggers volume disc). Two accounts: bill ACC-1001, ship ACC-1004 -> use ACC-1001. Enterprise, post-discount 17099.83 > 10000 -> ESCALATE.

case02.note: 'a dozen' -> 12 (<20, no volume disc). 'our enterprise plan' is narrative; tier from lookup is Standard -> no tier disc -> STANDARD.

case03.note: Enterprise, explicit qty 3. Subtotal 7500, tier disc 5% -> 7125 < 10000 -> STANDARD. AND-edge.

case04.note: Quantity unstated -> 1. 'please escalate!' is a narrative red herring; Free tier -> STANDARD.

case05.note: 'two dozen' -> 24 (volume disc). Enterprise (tier disc). Post-discount 51300 > 10000 -> ESCALATE. Long-range stacking.
